sections.title: Digital Operational Resilience Act (EU 2022/2554)
sections.content: DORA enforces a unified, pan-European approach to operational resilience. Financial entities must demonstrate the capability to withstand, respond to, and recover from all types of ICT-related disruptions and threats.

sections.title: ICT Asset Criticality & Risk Management
sections.content: Scoping must strictly map to the ICT Asset Register. Every in-scope asset is evaluated against 'Critical or Important Functions' (CIFs). Assets supporting CIFs require rigorous continuous testing, monitoring, and Board-level reporting.

sections.title: Third-Party ICT Risk Oversight
sections.content: Testing explicitly encompasses critical third-party ICT service providers (CTPPs). Financial entities are accountable for the security posture of their entire supply chain, mandating authorization and joint-testing protocols.

sections.title: Threat-Led Penetration Testing (TLPT)
sections.content: Advanced TLPT engagements are required to mirror the TTPs of advanced persistent threats (APTs) targeting the EU financial sector. Scenarios must be bespoke, intelligence-driven, and validated by the relevant competent authorities.

sections.title: Information Security Booklet (IT Examination Handbook)
sections.content: FFIEC scoping mandates a robust, risk-based approach to evaluating the institution's information security program, ensuring alignment with the Cybersecurity Assessment Tool (CAT) and enterprise risk management.

sections.title: Architecture, Infrastructure, and Operations (AIO)
sections.content: Assessments must cover the design and operation of network architectures, cloud environments, and hybrid infrastructures, ensuring that defense-in-depth principles are rigorously applied and maintained.

sections.title: Independent Control Validation
sections.content: Testing must be performed by independent parties without conflicts of interest in the design, installation, or maintenance of the systems being evaluated, ensuring objective regulatory examination readiness.

sections.title: Initial Access & Execution Strategies
sections.content: Scenarios evaluate adversarial techniques such as Spearphishing (T1566), Valid Accounts (T1078), and External Remote Services (T1133) to breach the perimeter and establish initial footholds.

sections.title: Defense Evasion & Persistence
sections.content: Testing validates the SOC's ability to detect advanced evasion techniques, including Process Injection (T1055), Obfuscated Files/Information (T1027), and Scheduled Task/Job manipulation (T1053).

sections.title: Credential Access & Lateral Movement
sections.content: Assessments simulate attempts to dump credentials (T1003) and move laterally via SMB/Windows Admin Shares (T1021.002) and Pass the Hash (T1550.002), challenging internal zero-trust boundaries.

sections.title: Command and Control (C2) & Exfiltration
sections.content: Validation of egress controls against Application Layer Protocol (T1071) C2 channels and automated Data Exfiltration (T1048), ensuring sensitive client data remains contained.

sections.title: A01:2021 - Broken Access Control
sections.content: Rigorous testing to ensure cryptographic boundaries and authorization schemas prevent horizontal and vertical privilege escalation, unauthorized data access, and API manipulation.

sections.title: A02:2021 - Cryptographic Failures
sections.content: Verification of data-at-rest and data-in-transit encryption standards, evaluating TLS configurations, key management lifecycle, and protection against cryptographic downgrades.

sections.title: A03:2021 - Injection
sections.content: Validation against payload injection vulnerabilities, including SQL, NoSQL, ORM, and OS Command injections, ensuring input sanitization and parameterized query enforcement.

sections.title: A04:2021 - Insecure Design & Architecture
sections.content: Threat modeling and architectural review to identify inherent design flaws that cannot be mitigated through patching, emphasizing secure-by-design principles early in the SDLC.

sections.title: Implementation Group 3 (IG3) Baselines
sections.content: Scoping aligns with CIS IG3, designed for mature organizations targeted by sophisticated adversaries, demanding the highest level of comprehensive cyber defense implementation.

sections.title: Control 1 & 2: Enterprise Asset and Software Inventory
sections.content: Active management, discovery, and tracking of all connected enterprise assets and software to eliminate shadow IT and establish a definitive attack surface baseline.

sections.title: Control 4: Secure Configuration
sections.content: Establishment and continuous enforcement of hardened secure configurations across enterprise assets, cloud infrastructure, and software applications.

sections.title: CBEST Intelligence-Led Testing Framework
sections.content: Testing must be driven by bespoke, high-fidelity threat intelligence targeting the Critical Business Services (CBS) of UK financial institutions, complying with PRA and FCA joint guidelines.

sections.title: STAR-FS & Supervisory Statements
sections.content: Alignment with SS1/21 (Operational resilience: Impact tolerances) to ensure the institution can remain within its impact tolerances during severe but plausible cyber attack scenarios.

sections.title: Systemic Risk & Contagion Assessment
sections.content: Simulations must model the potential for systemic contagion affecting the wider UK financial system, validating segregation controls and recovery playbooks.

sections.title: Cybersecurity Fortification Initiative (CFI) 2.0
sections.content: Comprehensive alignment with the HKMA's updated CFI 2.0 requirements, ensuring the Authorized Institution (AI) maintains robust cyber resilience.

sections.title: iCAST Execution & 'Assume Breach'
sections.content: Simulations must encompass the Intelligence-led Cyber Attack Simulation Testing (iCAST) methodology, utilizing 'Assume Breach' paradigms to stress-test the SOC's detection, response, and containment capabilities.

sections.title: Cyber Resilience Assessment Framework (C-RAF)
sections.content: Scoping supports the C-RAF inherent risk assessment, calibrating the depth of technical validation based on the institution's designated risk profile (Low, Medium, or High).

sections.title: SR 13-1 / Third-Party Risk Management
sections.content: Strict scoping inclusion of critical third-party vendors and external-facing infrastructure, aligning with the Federal Reserve Board's expectations for comprehensive vendor risk oversight.

sections.title: SR 14-8 / Consolidated Recovery Planning
sections.content: Validation that the organization's technical controls support its consolidated recovery plans, ensuring the ability to resume core business lines under severe cyber distress.

sections.title: Sound Practices for Cyber Resilience
sections.content: Evaluation of the institution's alignment with interagency guidelines on cyber resilience, focusing on governance, dependency management, and incident response readiness.

sections.title: Technology Risk Management (TRM) Guidelines
sections.content: Alignment with the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) TRM guidelines. Scoping includes risk assessments across IT infrastructure, applications, and third-party vendor integrations.

sections.title: Adversarial Attack Simulation (AASE)
sections.content: Simulations encompass threat-led penetration testing aligned with MAS AASE requirements, targeting critical business functions and assessing the institution's capability to detect and respond to advanced cyber threats.

sections.title: Cyber Hygiene Notice (Notice 655)
sections.content: Technical validation of the institution's adherence to baseline cyber hygiene practices, including administrative account security, malware protection, and network segmentation.

sections.title: CPS 234 Information Security Standard
sections.content: Comprehensive testing to ensure the APRA-regulated entity maintains an information security capability commensurate with the size and extent of threats to its information assets.

sections.title: CORIE Framework Testing
sections.content: Execution of Cyber Operational Resilience Intelligence-led Exercises (CORIE) to evaluate the detection and response maturity of the financial institution against targeted cyber attacks.

sections.title: Third-Party Information Asset Protection
sections.content: Rigorous scope inclusion of external supply chain and related third parties that manage the institution's information assets, fulfilling APRA's third-party risk management expectations.

sections.title: 23 NYCRR Part 500 Compliance
sections.content: Verification of the Covered Entity's cybersecurity program as required by the New York State Department of Financial Services, specifically evaluating penetration testing and vulnerability management practices.

sections.title: Multi-Factor Authentication & Access Controls
sections.content: Detailed technical assessment of non-public information (NPI) boundaries, evaluating the robustness of MFA implementations for external and internal access as mandated by the amended regulations.

sections.title: Incident Response Plan Readiness
sections.content: Simulations designed to trigger and test the efficacy of the organization's incident response procedures, specifically focusing on containment times and regulatory notification triggers.
